title: SDS-PAGE protein gel + Coomassie stain
description: Run an SDS-PAGE protein gel and Coomassie stain it, following Bio-Rad Mini-PROTEAN and Bio-Safe Coomassie protocols.

payload.body:
## Purpose

Separate denatured proteins by molecular weight on an SDS-PAGE gel and stain the gel directly with Coomassie. This is a standalone protein gel (no transfer, no antibodies).

## Materials

| Item | Amount |
| --- | --- |
| SDS-PAGE gel (e.g. Mini-PROTEAN TGX, 7.5-12% or 4-20% gradient) | 1 gel |
| 4X Laemmli sample buffer + reducing agent (2-mercaptoethanol or DTT) | per sample |
| 1X Tris/glycine/SDS (TGS) running buffer | about 750 mL |
| Protein MW ladder | 1 lane |
| Bio-Safe Coomassie G-250 stain | about 50 mL per gel |
| Deionized water (ddH2O) | for washes and destain |

## Gel percentage vs protein size (Bio-Rad TGX)

| Gel | Range (kDa) |
| --- | --- |
| 7.5% | 40-200 |
| 10% | 30-150 |
| 12% | 20-120 |
| 4-20% gradient | about 10-200 |

## Steps

1. Optional: clarify the lysate by centrifuging 10-15 min at over 12,000 x g, 20 C.
2. Mix each sample with 4X Laemmli buffer plus reducing agent.
3. Heat at 95 C for 5 min (or 70 C for 10 min).
4. Prepare 1X TGS (100 mL 10X TGS plus 900 mL water). Fill the inner chamber with about 200 mL and the outer chamber to the 2-gel line.
5. Load 15-20 ug total protein per lane plus a ladder.
6. Run 50 V for 10 min, then 200 V until the bromophenol blue front reaches the bottom (about 35 min total for the run).
7. Wash the gel 3 x 5 min in about 200 mL ddH2O to remove SDS.
8. Add about 50 mL Bio-Safe Coomassie (enough to cover) and shake gently 1 h. Bands appear within 20 min.
9. Destain in about 200 mL ddH2O for at least 30 min. The gel can be stored in water.

## Expected result

Blue protein bands at the expected molecular weights, with detection down to about 8 ng per band.

## Notes and troubleshooting

- Choose a percentage where the target protein lands in the lower half of the gel.
- Set the reducing agent amount (2-mercaptoethanol or DTT) per your sample-buffer recipe.
- Faint bands: load more protein and avoid over-destaining, which fades weak bands.

## Safety

Unpolymerized acrylamide is a neurotoxin; prefer precast gels and wear gloves. 2-mercaptoethanol is toxic and malodorous; work in a fume hood.

## Source

Gel run adapted from Bio-Rad Mini-PROTEAN protein electrophoresis guidance; staining from the Bio-Rad Bio-Safe Coomassie Stain bulletin.
https://www.bio-rad.com/en-us/applications-technologies/performing-protein-electrophoresis?ID=LUSPFBNEL
https://www.bio-rad.com/webroot/web/pdf/lsr/literature/Bulletin_2423.pdf
